mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate below 0.5% signals an ultra-loose monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset price multiples. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate remains in the zero-bound zone.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or forward returns in zero-bound periods fail to exceed baseline by more than 2% annualized (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 9% signals labor market slack and economic weakness that depresses corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when joblessness exceeds this critical level.
regime_hint: labor market recession or post-shock recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 9%, or Rank IC between UNRATE and 5-day forward returns is positive and > 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales decline of more than 2% month-over-month signals deteriorating consumer demand and forward economic contraction. This interaction-driven mechanism combines retail sales weakness with elevated unemployment to amplify the negative equity return signal over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS=363704M and UNRATE=9.6% suggest demand weakness
failure_condition: Joint signal (RSAFS decline + UNRATE elevation) produces negative 20-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of forward return sign on both proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index below 93.5 indicates supply-side contraction and reduced productive capacity. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days during supply-constrained regimes where input costs are rising and output growth is stalling.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO and 60-day forward returns is positive or near-zero (> -0.02) over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in INDPRO < 93.5 regimes exceed baseline by > 1% annualized (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 550 thousand units annualized signal weak residential investment and forward demand contraction. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when housing construction falls below this level, indicating reduced future consumer wealth and spending.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances when HOUST < 550, or Rank IC between HOUST and 20-day forward returns is positive and > 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth below 100 thousand persons per month signals labor market deceleration and forward economic weakness. This interaction-driven mechanism combines weak payroll growth with rising unemployment to predict negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS=130311k with UNRATE=9.6% indicates labor market stress
failure_condition: Joint signal (weak PAYEMS + high UNRATE) produces negative 20-day returns in < 52% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in logistic regression of forward return sign.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI above 217.5 (index level) combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in high-inflation, high-rates regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:current CPIAUCSL=217.597 and FEDFUNDS=0.19% indicate low inflation pressure currently
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: Joint signal (high CPI + elevated FEDFUNDS) produces negative 60-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.15) in logistic regression of forward return sign on both proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 above 13200 billion chained dollars signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days during periods of above-trend real output growth.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1=13191.5 is near trend level
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 and 60-day forward returns is negative or near-zero (< 0.02) over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in high-GDPC1 periods do not exceed baseline by > 1.5% annualized (t-test p > 0.12).
